label_suggestions.name: cloud-ok
label_suggestions.description: Issue can be validated, fixed, and re-verified in Codex cloud from repo state plus setup.

label_suggestions.name: local-only
label_suggestions.description: Issue depends on local-only assets, device-specific state, or context not reproducible in Codex cloud.

label_suggestions.name: needs-acceptance-proof
label_suggestions.description: Issue can be fixed in cloud but still needs a final local/browser-backed acceptance recording.

current_batches.description: First cloud wave: Block Crush transition polish and dialogue streaming after the initial readability pass landed.
Independent remote-first tooling and asset-foundation work that can run in parallel with the client-shell priority wave.
Independent remote-first tooling and asset-foundation work that can run in parallel with the client-shell priority wave.
Second cloud wave: follow-up onboarding, HUD, Block Crush guidance, and tap-flow fixes after the priority wave settles.
Later follow-up cleanup after the focused fixes land, including residual readability audit work.

issue_overrides.reason: Onboarding clarity changes are code-only and reproducible from repo state.
The requested looping backdrop and avatar work are asset-dependent until shared asset hosting exists.
HUD discoverability is a repo-contained UI/task copy problem.
PR #33 landed the first readability pass on March 14, 2026, so the remaining scope is a lower-priority audit and mobile-device follow-up rather than the next blocking fix.
The wasted-tap fix can be developed in cloud, but final confidence depends on timed browser tracing.
The transition bug is reproducible in cloud, but a fixed claim still needs reviewer-visible temporal evidence from the real Act 2 flow rather than a hidden local artifact path.
Streaming behavior remains in the priority wave, but it should follow #18 so the client-shell queue stays serialized while the final player-visible result still gets browser-backed verification.
The Block Crush first-time hint and early-stage difficulty tuning are lightweight UI/gameplay follow-ups that fit the second wave.
